title: Installing Node.js and npm
description: Get Node.js on your machine and verify the installation.

slides.title: Installation Options 💻
slides.content:
# Installing Node.js

There are two main ways to install Node.js:

1. **Official installer** from [nodejs.org](https://nodejs.org)
   - Choose the **LTS** (Long‑Term Support) version for most projects.
   - The installer includes **npm** (Node Package Manager).

2. **Version manager** (recommended for developers)
   - **nvm** (Node Version Manager) on macOS/Linux
   - **nvm-windows** on Windows
   - Allows switching between Node versions easily.

We'll cover the official installer first, then mention version managers.

slides.title: Step‑by‑Step (Official Installer)
slides.content:
## Using the Official Installer

1. Go to [nodejs.org](https://nodejs.org).
2. Download the LTS version for your operating system.
3. Run the installer (accept defaults – they're safe).
4. Once finished, open a terminal (Command Prompt, PowerShell, or terminal app).
5. Verify the installation with:
   ```bash
   node -v
   npm -v
   ```

You should see version numbers like `v18.17.0` and `9.6.7`. Congratulations – Node.js is ready!

slides.title: Verification in Action
slides.content: Open your terminal and type these commands. The output should look similar:

quizData.question: Which command shows the installed Node.js version?
quizData.explanation: Both `node -v` and `node --version` work. They are aliases.

slides.title: Version Managers (Advanced) 🔧
slides.content:
## Why use a version manager?

Different projects may require different Node.js versions. A version manager lets you switch seamlessly.

### nvm (macOS/Linux)
```bash
curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.5/install.sh | bash
# then
nvm install 18   # install latest v18
nvm use 18       # use it
```

### nvm-windows
Download from [nvm-windows releases](https://github.com/coreybutler/nvm-windows/releases).

After installing, you can switch versions with `nvm use <version>`.

slides.title: ⚠️ Permission Issues
slides.content:
## Avoid permission headaches

If you install Node.js globally on macOS/Linux without a version manager, you might need `sudo` for global npm packages. This can lead to permission errors.

**Solution:** Use a version manager like nvm – it installs Node in your home directory, so no `sudo` needed.
